title: Lindsay Clancy murder trial: Judge limits access to autopsy photos, husband’s 911 call
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime The public is barred from recording or streaming the Clancy children’s autopsy photos and the 911 call Patrick Clancy made the night of the killings.
Lindsay Clancy sits as jury selection starts in her murder trial on Monday, July 20, 2026, in Plymouth Superior Court.

Greg Derr / The Patriot Ledger via AP, Pool By Abby Patkin July 24, 2026 | 1:32 PM 1 minute to read The judge overseeing Lindsay Clancy’s murder trial has barred media outlets from recording or livestreaming the anguished 911 call Clancy’s then-husband made the night she allegedly killed their children.

title: New court docs reveal how scammers allegedly impersonated Concord landowners, secretly selling their property
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News A Middleborough man and others face criminal charges for impersonating property owners in four states and fraudulently selling their land.
In 2024, developers began construction of this house on Mattison Drive in Concord before finding out that the true property owners had never actually sold it in the first place.
Handout courtesy Richard Vetstein ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: MassDOT unveils new renderings of the future Sagamore Bridge
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The replacement bridge will feature two spans, a sunset overlook, wider shoulders, and a separated path for pedestrians and cyclists.
A July 2026 rendering of the Sagamore Bridge replacement.
MassDOT Another summer Friday, another backup at the Sagamore Bridge.

title: ‘There is no longer any mystery’: Prosecutors say DNA helped solve young mother’s 1995 slaying
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime “Nothing we announce today can return Celestina to the children who grew up without their mother or erase the grief her family has carried for 31 years.

” By Abby Patkin July 24, 2026 | 12:42 PM 2 minutes to read More than three decades after Celestina Perez was found strangled in a Springfield park, advances in DNA technology have helped investigators identify the young mother’s killer.
Hampden District Attorney Anthony Gulluni announced Wednesday that it was Joseph James Ceravolo Jr.

title: Police identify victim in Boston Common homicide
author: John Waller
bullets: Local News The 35-year-old Norwood man was pronounced dead after being found "suffering from trauma" early Wednesday morning.
The scene on Wednesday.

Bryan Hecht for The Boston Globe By John Waller July 24, 2026 | 12:21 PM 1 minute to read Police have identified the man who was pronounced dead Wednesday after being found “suffering from trauma” near the intersection of Boylston and Tremont streets on the Boston Common early that morning.

title: Boston Globe sports columnist Chris Gasper returns to work after prostate cancer surgery
author: John Waller
bullets: Media "Covering sports was always my dream.
